page: Amazon Web Services c7i.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. The instance provides 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled, resulting in 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it features a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1938.18 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1665.0. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively weak. The stress-ng multi-core score is 1991.39, and the Geekbench multi-core score is 2034.0. Memory bandwidth tests show poor performance, with read-only large block speeds at 11.05 GB/sec and write large block speeds at 11.76 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for deep learning and matrix operations, though LLM inference on a small 135M model remains modest at 54.88 tokens per second for text generation.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c7i.large offers a low-cost entry point into Intel Gen7 compute-optimized family, utilizing high-speed DDR5 memory and dedicated CPU resources. However, the limited 4.0 GB memory capacity and weak multi-core scaling present a tradeoff for memory-intensive or highly parallelized applications. This instance is best suited for single-threaded or light compute workloads, basic web serving, text processing, and development tasks that benefit from modern Intel instruction sets but do not require high memory density or local storage.
description: The Amazon Web Services c7i.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it lacks local storage and GPUs, offering a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmarks show average single-core performance, including a Geekbench single-core score of 1665.0 and solid single-threaded compression results, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are weak. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. This instance provides a cost-effective entry point for compute-focused workloads but is constrained by its low memory capacity. It is best suited for light compute tasks, single-threaded applications, text processing, and basic web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.large is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 2 dedicated vCPUs, and 4.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, running on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.large is a compute-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A compute-optimized entry-level instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing, DDR5 memory, and advanced vector extensions for single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 2 vCPUs
Equipped with 4.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Delivers average single-core performance but limited multi-core scaling
Configured with no local storage and a 1 Gbps baseline network speed
Best suited for light compute, single-threaded tasks, and text processing
